questions.domain: 領域 2：設計高性能架構 (Design High-Performing Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司在大量 Amazon EC2 实例上运行应用程序。该应用程序读取 Amazon DynamoDB 表中的条目并将其写入其中。 DynamoDB 表的大小不断增长，但应用程序只需要最近 30 天的数据。该公司需要一种能够最大限度地减少成本和开发工作的解决方案。哪种解决方案满足这些要求？
question_text.en: A company runs an application on a large fleet of Amazon EC2 instances. The application reads and writes entries into an Amazon DynamoDB table. The size of the DynamoDB table continuously grows, but the application needs only data from the last 30 days. The company needs a solution that minimizes cost and development effort. Which solution meets these requirements?

A.zh: 使用 AWS CloudFormation 模板部署完整的解决方案。每 30 天重新部署一次 CloudFormation 堆栈，并删除原始堆栈。
A.en: Use an AWS CloudFormation template to deploy the complete solution. Redeploy the CloudFormation stack every 30 days, and delete the original stack.

B.zh: 使用运行来自 AWS Marketplace 的监控应用程序的 EC2 实例。设置监控应用程序以使用 Amazon DynamoDB Streams 来保存在表格中创建新项目时的时间戳记。使用在 EC2 实例上运行的脚本来删除时间戳早于 30 天的项目。
B.en: Use an EC2 instance that runs a monitoring application from AWS Marketplace. Configure the monitoring application to use Amazon DynamoDB Streams to store the timestamp when a new item is created in the table. Use a script that runs on the EC2 instance to delete items that have a timestamp that is older than 30 days.

C.zh: 设置 Amazon DynamoDB Streams 以在表格中创建新项目时调用 AWS Lambda 函数。配置 Lambda 函数以删除表中超过 30 天的项目。
C.en: Configure Amazon DynamoDB Streams to invoke an AWS Lambda function when a new item is created in the table. Configure the Lambda function to delete items in the table that are older than 30 days.

D.zh: 扩展应用程序，向表中创建的每个新项目添加一个属性，该属性的值是当前时间戳加上 30 天。配置 DynamoDB 以使用该属性作为 TTL 属性。
D.en: Extend the application to add an attribute that has a value of the current timestamp plus 30 days to each new item that is created in the table. Configure DynamoDB to use the attribute as the TTL attribute.

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因： CloudFormation主要用于资源的自动化部署和管理，而非数据管理。重新部署整个堆栈，删除原来的堆栈，效率低下，不符合「最小化开发工作量」的要求。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: CloudFormation is mainly used for automated deployment and management of resources, not data management. Redeploying the entire stack and deleting the original stack is inefficient and does not meet the requirements of "minimizing development effort.".

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：此方法需要额外的 EC2 实例和监控应用程序，从而增加了成本和复杂性。手动运行脚本也增加了开发工作量，不符合「成本和开发工作量最小化」的要求。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This approach requires additional EC2 instances and monitoring applications, adding cost and complexity. Manually running scripts also increases the development workload and does not meet the requirement of "minimizing costs and development workload".

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：尽管可以使用 Lambda 函数自动运行删除操作，但需要额外的开发工作来编写和维护 Lambda 函数逻辑。与选项D相比，开发工作量更大。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Although deletion operations can be automated using Lambda functions, additional development effort is required to write and maintain Lambda function logic. Compared with option D, the development workload is greater.

D.zh: 正確。技术原因： DynamoDB 的 TTL 功能会自动删除过期数据，无需额外的开发工作或资源。通过设置 TTL 属性， DynamoDB 将自动清理超过 30 天的数据，满足「最小化成本和开发工作量」的要求。
D.en: Correct. Technical reason: DynamoDB's TTL feature automatically deletes expired data without requiring additional development work or resources. By setting the TTL attribute, DynamoDB will automatically clean data older than 30 days, meeting the requirement of "minimizing costs and development effort.".

answer_text.zh: D. 扩展应用程序，向表中创建的每个新项目添加一个属性，该属性的值是当前时间戳加上 30 天。配置 DynamoDB 以使用该属性作为 TTL 属性。
answer_text.en: D. Extend the application to add an attribute that has a value of the current timestamp plus 30 days to each new item that is created in the table. Configure DynamoDB to use the attribute as the TTL attribute.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果： D (95%) 5%。热门讨论与考点摘要： DynamoDB 的 TTL 功能会自动删除过期数据，无需额外的开发工作或资源。通过设置 TTL 属性， DynamoDB 将自动清理超过 30 天的数据，满足「最小化成本和开发工作量」的要求。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: D (95%) 5%. Popular discussion and key point summary: DynamoDB's TTL feature automatically deletes expired data without requiring additional development work or resources. By setting the TTL attribute, DynamoDB will automatically clean data older than 30 days, meeting the requirement of "minimizing costs and development effort.".

questions.domain: 領域 1：設計彈性架構 (Design Resilient Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司有一个在本地 Windows Server 上运行的 Microsoft .NET 应用程序。该应用程序使用 Oracle 数据库标准版服务器存储数据。该公司正在计划迁移到 AWS，并希望在迁移应用程序时最大程度地减少开发变更。 AWS应用程序环境应该是高可用的。公司应该采取哪些行动组合来满足这些要求？ （选两个。）
question_text.en: A company has a Microsoft .NET application that runs on an on-premises Windows Server. The application stores data by using an Oracle Database Standard Edition server. The company is planning a migration to AWS and wants to minimize development changes while moving the application. The AWS application environment should be highly available. Which combination of actions should the company take to meet these requirements? (Choose two.)

A.zh: 使用运行 .NET Core 的 AWS Lambda 函数将应用程序重构为无服务器。
A.en: Refactor the application as serverless with AWS Lambda functions running .NET Core.

B.zh: 在多可用区部署中使用 .NET 平台重新托管 AWS Elastic Beanstalk 中的应用程序。
B.en: Rehost the application in AWS Elastic Beanstalk with the .NET platform in a Multi-AZ deployment.

C.zh: 重新构建应用程序平台，以便使用 Amazon Linux Amazon 机器映像 (AMI) 在 Amazon EC2 上运行。
C.en: Replatform the application to run on Amazon EC2 with the Amazon Linux Amazon Machine Image (AMI).

D.zh: 使用 AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS) 在多可用区部署中从 Oracle 数据库迁移到 Amazon DynamoDB。
D.en: Use AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS) to migrate from the Oracle database to Amazon DynamoDB in a Multi-AZ deployment.

E.zh: 使用 AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS) 在多可用区部署中从 Oracle 数据库迁移到 Amazon RDS 上的 Oracle。
E.en: Use AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS) to migrate from the Oracle database to Oracle on Amazon RDS in a Multi- AZ deployment.

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：将应用程序重构为无服务器架构需要大量的开发工作，这与问题所要求的「最小化开发变更」不一致。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Refactoring the application to a serverless architecture requires a lot of development work, which is inconsistent with the "minimized development changes" required by the question.

B.zh: 正確。技术原因： AWS Elastic Beanstalk 支持.NET 平台快速迁移应用程序，并支持多可用区部署以实现高可用性。
B.en: Correct. Technical reason: AWS Elastic Beanstalk supports the .NET platform to quickly migrate applications and supports multi-AZ deployment for high availability.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：问题提到应用程序在 Windows Server 上运行，但 Amazon Linux AMI 不支持 Windows 环境。迁移到 Linux 需要大量的开发工作。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: The question mentions that the application runs on Windows Server, but the Amazon Linux AMI does not support the Windows environment. Migrating to Linux requires a lot of development work.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因： DynamoDB 是一种 NoSQL 数据库，与现有的 Oracle 数据库（关系数据库）架构不兼容。迁移到 DynamoDB 需要对应用程序进行大量修改。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: DynamoDB is a NoSQL database and is incompatible with the existing Oracle database (relational database) architecture. Migrating to DynamoDB requires extensive modifications to the application.

E.zh: 正確。技术原因： Amazon RDS 支持 Oracle 数据库，可使用 AWS DMS 进行迁移，并支持多可用区部署以实现高可用性。
E.en: Correct. Technical reason: Amazon RDS supports Oracle databases, can be migrated using AWS DMS, and supports multi-AZ deployment for high availability.

answer_text.zh: B. 在多可用区部署中使用 .NET 平台重新托管 AWS Elastic Beanstalk 中的应用程序。；E. 使用 AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS) 在多可用区部署中从 Oracle 数据库迁移到 Amazon RDS 上的 Oracle。
answer_text.en: B. Rehost the application in AWS Elastic Beanstalk with the .NET platform in a Multi-AZ deployment.; E. Use AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS) to migrate from the Oracle database to Oracle on Amazon RDS in a Multi- AZ deployment.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果： BE (97%) 3%。热门讨论与考点摘要： AWS Elastic Beanstalk 支持.NET 平台快速迁移应用程序，并支持多可用区部署以实现高可用性。； Amazon RDS 支持 Oracle 数据库，可使用 AWS DMS 进行迁移，并支持多可用区部署以实现高可用性。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: BE (97%) 3%. Popular discussion and key point summary: AWS Elastic Beanstalk supports the .NET platform to quickly migrate applications and supports multi-AZ deployment for high availability.; Amazon RDS supports Oracle databases, can be migrated using AWS DMS, and supports multi-AZ deployment for high availability.

questions.domain: 領域 2：設計高性能架構 (Design High-Performing Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司在本地数据中心的 Kubernetes 集群上运行容器化应用程序。该公司正在使用 MongoDB 数据库进行数据保存。该公司希望将其中一些环境迁移到 AWS，但目前无法更改代码或部署方法。该公司需要一种能够最大限度地减少运维开销的解决方案。哪种解决方案满足这些要求？
question_text.en: A company runs a containerized application on a Kubernetes cluster in an on-premises data center. The company is using a MongoDB database for data storage. The company wants to migrate some of these environments to AWS, but no code changes or deployment method changes are possible at this time. The company needs a solution that minimizes operational overhead. Which solution meets these requirements?

A.zh: 将 Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) 与 Amazon EC2 工作线程节点结合使用进行计算，并使用 EC2 上的 MongoDB 进行数据保存。
A.en: Use Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) with Amazon EC2 worker nodes for compute and MongoDB on EC2 for data storage.

B.zh: 将 Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) 与 AWS Fargate 结合使用进行计算，并使用 Amazon DynamoDB 进行数据存储
B.en: Use Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) with AWS Fargate for compute and Amazon DynamoDB for data storage

C.zh: 将 Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (Amazon EKS) 与 Amazon EC2 工作线程节点结合使用进行计算，并使用 Amazon DynamoDB 进行数据保存。
C.en: Use Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (Amazon EKS) with Amazon EC2 worker nodes for compute and Amazon DynamoDB for data storage.

D.zh: 将 Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (Amazon EKS) 与 AWS Fargate 结合使用进行计算，并使用 Amazon DocumentDB（与 MongoDB 兼容）进行数据保存。
D.en: Use Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (Amazon EKS) with AWS Fargate for compute and Amazon DocumentDB (with MongoDB compatibility) for data storage.

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因： Amazon ECS可以运行容器化应用程序，但使用EC2实例运行MongoDB会增加管理开销（如手动安装、维护、备份等）。原因 不符合「最小化操作开销」的要求。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: ness analysis: Amazon ECS can run containerized applications, but using EC2 instances to run MongoDB will increase management overhead (such as manual installation, maintenance, backup, etc.). Reason for the The requirement of "minimizing operation overhead" is not met.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因： AWS Fargate减少了管理计算资源的开销，但DynamoDB是NoSQL数据库，与MongoDB的兼容性较低。修改代码的原因是需要适配DynamoDB，不符合「不改变代码或部署方式」的要求。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: ness analysis: AWS Fargate reduces the overhead of managing computing resources, but DynamoDB is a NoSQL database and has low compatibility with MongoDB. Reason for the Code modification is required to adapt to DynamoDB, which does not meet the requirement of "no code or deployment method changes".

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因： Amazon EKS 支持 Kubernetes，但 DynamoDB 与 MongoDB 不兼容， EC2 工作节点增加了管理开销。原因 不符合「不更改代码」和「最小化操作开销」的要求。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: ness analysis: Amazon EKS supports Kubernetes, but DynamoDB is not compatible with MongoDB, and EC2 worker nodes increase management overhead. Reason for the The requirements of "no code changes" and "minimizing operational overhead" are not met.

D.zh: 正確。技术原因： Amazon EKS 支持 Kubernetes、AWS Fargate 减少计算资源管理开销、Amazon DocumentDB 兼容 MongoDB，并且符合「无代码变更」的要求。原因它完全满足问题要求，最大限度地减少操作开销，并且不需要更改代码或部署方法。
D.en: Correct. Technical reason: ness analysis: Amazon EKS supports Kubernetes, AWS Fargate reduces computing resource management overhead, Amazon DocumentDB is compatible with MongoDB, and meets the requirement of "no code changes". Reason why it is It fully meets the question requirements, minimizes operational overhead and requires no code or deployment method changes.

answer_text.zh: D. 将 Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (Amazon EKS) 与 AWS Fargate 结合使用进行计算，并使用 Amazon DocumentDB（与 MongoDB 兼容）进行数据保存。
answer_text.en: D. Use Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (Amazon EKS) with AWS Fargate for compute and Amazon DocumentDB (with MongoDB compatibility) for data storage.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果： D (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要： Amazon EKS 支持 Kubernetes、AWS Fargate 减少计算资源管理开销、Amazon DocumentDB 兼容 MongoDB，并且符合「无代码变更」的要求。原因它完全满足问题要求，最大限度地减少操作开销，并且不需要更改代码或部署方法。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: D (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: ness analysis: Amazon EKS supports Kubernetes, AWS Fargate reduces computing resource management overhead, Amazon DocumentDB is compatible with MongoDB, and meets the requirement of "no code changes". Reason why it is It fully meets the question requirements, minimizes operational overhead and requires no code or deployment method changes.

questions.domain: 領域 2：設計高性能架構 (Design High-Performing Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家电话行销公司正在 AWS 上设计其客户调用中心功能。该公司需要一种能够提供多个说话者识别并产生转录文档的解决方案。该公司想要查找记录文档以分析业务模式。成绩单文档必须保存 7 年以供审计之用。哪种解决方案可以满足这些要求？
question_text.en: A telemarketing company is designing its customer call center functionality on AWS. The company needs a solution that provides multiple speaker recognition and generates transcript files. The company wants to query the transcript files to analyze the business patterns. The transcript files must be stored for 7 years for auditing purposes. Which solution will meet these requirements?

A.zh: 使用 Amazon Rekognition 进行多说话人辨识。将转录文档保存在 Amazon S3 中。使用机器学习模型进行转录文档分析。
A.en: Use Amazon Rekognition for multiple speaker recognition. Store the transcript files in Amazon S3. Use machine learning models for transcript file analysis.

B.zh: 使用 Amazon Transcribe 进行多说话人辨识。使用 Amazon Athena 进行转录文档分析。
B.en: Use Amazon Transcribe for multiple speaker recognition. Use Amazon Athena for transcript file analysis.

C.zh: 使用 Amazon Translate 进行多说话人辨识。将转录文档保存在 Amazon Redshift 中。使用 SQL 查找进行转录文档分析。
C.en: Use Amazon Translate for multiple speaker recognition. Store the transcript files in Amazon Redshift. Use SQL queries for transcript file analysis.

D.zh: 使用 Amazon Rekognition 进行多说话人辨识。将转录文档保存在 Amazon S3 中。使用 Amazon Textract 进行转录文档分析。
D.en: Use Amazon Rekognition for multiple speaker recognition. Store the transcript files in Amazon S3. Use Amazon Textract for transcript file analysis.

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因： Amazon S3 可以保存脚本文档以满足长期保存需求。此题并未明确要求使用机器学习模型进行分析，也没有提及具体的分析工具。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Amazon S3 can store transcript files for long-term storage needs. The question does not clearly require the use of machine learning models for analysis, and no specific analysis tools are mentioned.

B.zh: 正確。技术原因： Amazon Athena 可以对 Amazon S3 中保存的脚本文档运行 SQL 查找分析。 Amazon S3 可满足您 7 年的长期保存需求。
B.en: Correct. Technical reason: Amazon Athena can perform SQL query analysis on transcript files stored in Amazon S3. Amazon S3 can meet your long-term storage needs for 7 years.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因： Amazon Redshift 是一项数据仓库服务。虽然它支持SQL查找，但它不适合长期保存转录文档。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Amazon Redshift is a data warehouse service. Although it supports SQL queries, it is not suitable for storing transcription files for long-term storage.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因： Amazon S3 可以保存脚本文档以满足长期保存需求。 Amazon Textract 用于从文档中提取文本消息，不适合分析转录文档。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Amazon S3 can store transcript files for long-term storage needs. Amazon Textract is used to extract text information from documents and is not suitable for analyzing transcribed files.

answer_text.zh: B. 使用 Amazon Transcribe 进行多说话人辨识。使用 Amazon Athena 进行转录文档分析。
answer_text.en: B. Use Amazon Transcribe for multiple speaker recognition. Use Amazon Athena for transcript file analysis.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果： B (93%) 4%。热门讨论与考点摘要： Amazon Athena 可以对 Amazon S3 中保存的脚本文档运行 SQL 查找分析。 Amazon S3 可满足您 7 年的长期保存需求。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: B (93%) 4%. Popular discussion and key point summary: Amazon Athena can perform SQL query analysis on transcript files stored in Amazon S3. Amazon S3 can meet your long-term storage needs for 7 years.

questions.domain: 領域 2：設計高性能架構 (Design High-Performing Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司在 AWS 上托管其应用程序。该公司使用 Amazon Cognito 来管理用户。当用户登录应用程序时，应用程序使用 Amazon API Gateway 中托管的 REST API 从 Amazon DynamoDB 获取所需数据。该公司需要一个 AWS 托管解决方案来控制对 REST API 的访问，以减少开发工作。哪种解决方案能够以最少的运维开销满足这些要求？
question_text.en: A company hosts its application on AWS. The company uses Amazon Cognito to manage users. When users log in to the application, the application fetches required data from Amazon DynamoDB by using a REST API that is hosted in Amazon API Gateway. The company wants an AWS managed solution that will control access to the REST API to reduce development efforts. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

A.zh: 将 AWS Lambda 函数设置为 API Gateway 中的授权者，以验证哪个用户发出了请求。
A.en: Configure an AWS Lambda function to be an authorizer in API Gateway to validate which user made the request.

B.zh: 为每个用户创建并分配一个必须随每个请求发送的 API 密钥。使用 AWS Lambda 函数验证密钥。
B.en: For each user, create and assign an API key that must be sent with each request. Validate the key by using an AWS Lambda function.

C.zh: 在每个请求的标头中发送用户的电子邮件地址。调用 AWS Lambda 函数来验证具有该电子邮件地址的用户是否具有正确的访问权限。
C.en: Send the user’s email address in the header with every request. Invoke an AWS Lambda function to validate that the user with that email address has proper access.

D.zh: 在 API Gateway 中设置 Amazon Cognito 用户池授权方，以允许 Amazon Cognito 验证每个要求。
D.en: Configure an Amazon Cognito user pool authorizer in API Gateway to allow Amazon Cognito to validate each request.

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因： Lambda授权方虽然可以实现用户认证，但需要自订代码的开发和维护，增加了运维工作量。这不符合「减少开发工作量」和「最小化运维开销」的要求问题。因此这个选项是错误的。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Analysis: Although the Lambda authorizer can implement user authentication, it requires the development and maintenance of custom code, which increases the operation and maintenance workload. This does not meet the "reduce development effort" and "minimize operation and maintenance overhead" requirements of the question. Therefore this option is wrong.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因： API密钥的管理和分发需要额外的开发工作，且API密钥本身不提供用户认证功能。还需要额外的Lambda函数来验证密钥，增加了运维的复杂度。因此这个选项是错误的。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Analysis: The management and distribution of API keys requires additional development work, and API keys themselves do not provide user authentication functionality. Additional Lambda functions are also needed to verify the key, which increases the complexity of operation and maintenance. Therefore this option is wrong.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：访问权限。分析：这种方式需要开发和维护自订的Lambda函数来验证用户权限，直接发送用户的电子邮件地址可能会带来安全风险。这不符合「减少开发工作量」和「最小化运维开销」的要求问题。因此这个选项是错误的。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Access rights. Analysis: This approach requires developing and maintaining a custom Lambda function to verify user permissions, and sending the user's email address directly may pose security risks. This does not meet the "reduce development effort" and "minimize operation and maintenance overhead" requirements of the question. Therefore this option is wrong.

D.zh: 正確。技术原因： Amazon Cognito 用户池授权器是 AWS 托管解决方案，可直接与 API Gateway 集成以自动处理用户验证和授权。此方法减少了开发工作量和运维开销，完全满足问题的要求。因此这个选项是。
D.en: Correct. Technical reason: Analysis: Amazon Cognito User Pool Authorizer is an AWS managed solution that can be integrated directly with API Gateway to automatically handle user authentication and authorization. This method reduces development workload and operation and maintenance overhead, and fully meets the requirements of the question. Therefore this option is.

answer_text.zh: D. 在 API Gateway 中设置 Amazon Cognito 用户池授权方，以允许 Amazon Cognito 验证每个要求。
answer_text.en: D. Configure an Amazon Cognito user pool authorizer in API Gateway to allow Amazon Cognito to validate each request.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果： D (98%) 2%。热门讨论与考点摘要：分析： Amazon Cognito 用户池授权器是 AWS 托管解决方案，可直接与 API Gateway 集成以自动处理用户验证和授权。此方法减少了开发工作量和运维开销，完全满足问题的要求。因此这个选项是。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: D (98%) 2%. Popular discussion and key point summary: Analysis: Amazon Cognito User Pool Authorizer is an AWS managed solution that can be integrated directly with API Gateway to automatically handle user authentication and authorization. This method reduces development workload and operation and maintenance overhead, and fully meets the requirements of the question. Therefore this option is.

questions.domain: 領域 2：設計高性能架構 (Design High-Performing Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司正在开发针对行动应用程序用户的行销传播服务。该公司需要通过短信服务 (SMS) 向用户发送确认消息。用户必须能够回复短信。该公司必须将回复保存一年以供分析。解决方案架构师应该怎么做才能满足这些要求？
question_text.en: A company is developing a marketing communications service that targets mobile app users. The company needs to send confirmation messages with Short Message Service (SMS) to its users. The users must be able to reply to the SMS messages. The company must store the responses for a year for analysis. What should a solutions architect do to meet these requirements?

A.zh: 创建 Amazon Connect 联系流以发送 SMS 消息。使用 AWS Lambda 处理回应。
A.en: Create an Amazon Connect contact flow to send the SMS messages. Use AWS Lambda to process the responses.

B.zh: 建构 Amazon Pinpoint 旅程。配置 Amazon Pinpoint 以将事件发送至 Amazon Kinesis 数据流以进行分析和归档。
B.en: Build an Amazon Pinpoint journey. Configure Amazon Pinpoint to send events to an Amazon Kinesis data stream for analysis and archiving.

C.zh: 使用 Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) 散布 SMS 消息。使用 AWS Lambda 处理回应。
C.en: Use Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) to distribute the SMS messages. Use AWS Lambda to process the responses.

D.zh: 创建 Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) FIFO 主题。将 Amazon Kinesis 数据流订阅到 SNS 主题以进行分析和归档。
D.en: Create an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) FIFO topic. Subscribe an Amazon Kinesis data stream to the SNS topic for analysis and archiving.

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因： Amazon Connect 主要用于构建调用中心解决方案，而不是发送 SMS 消息。虽然可以使用 AWS Lambda 处理回应，但 Amazon Connect 不适合这种情况。原因 此选项无法满足发送短信和保存回复的需求。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Amazon Connect is primarily intended for building call center solutions rather than sending SMS messages. While it is possible to use AWS Lambda to handle responses, Amazon Connect is not suitable for this scenario. Reason for This option does not meet the needs of sending SMS messages and storing replies.

B.zh: 正確。技术原因：此选项完全满足发送短信、处理回复和存储数据的需求。
B.en: Correct. Technical reason: reason: This option fully meets the needs of sending SMS messages, processing replies, and storing data.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：回应。 Amazon SQS 主要用于消息排队，而不是直接发送 SMS 消息。虽然可以使用 AWS Lambda 处理回复，但 SQS 不适合发送 SMS 消息。原因 此选项不符合发送 SMS 消息的要求。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: responses. Amazon SQS is primarily used for message queuing rather than sending SMS messages directly. While it is possible to use AWS Lambda to handle replies, SQS is not suitable for sending SMS messages. Reason for the This option does not meet the requirements for sending SMS messages.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因： SNS 主题进行分析与归档。 Amazon SNS 可以发送通知，但不支持用户回复 SMS 消息。 FIFO主题主要用于严格的消息排序，而不是SMS消息发送。原因 此选项不支持用户回复短信的需要。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: the SNS topic for analysis and archiving. Amazon SNS can send notifications, but does not support users replying to SMS messages. FIFO topics are mainly used for strict message ordering, not SMS message sending. Reason for This option does not support the user's need to reply to SMS messages.

answer_text.zh: B. 建构 Amazon Pinpoint 旅程。配置 Amazon Pinpoint 以将事件发送至 Amazon Kinesis 数据流以进行分析和归档。
answer_text.en: B. Build an Amazon Pinpoint journey. Configure Amazon Pinpoint to send events to an Amazon Kinesis data stream for analysis and archiving.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果： B (90%) 7%。热门讨论与考点摘要：原因：此选项完全满足发送短信、处理回复和存储数据的需求。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: B (90%) 7%. Popular discussion and key point summary: reason: This option fully meets the needs of sending SMS messages, processing replies, and storing data.

questions.domain: 領域 3：設計安全架構 (Design Secure Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司计划将其数据移至 Amazon S3 存储桶。数据保存在S3存储桶中时必须进行加密。此外，加密密钥必须每年自动轮换。哪种解决方案能够以最少的运维开销满足这些要求？
question_text.en: A company is planning to move its data to an Amazon S3 bucket. The data must be encrypted when it is stored in the S3 bucket. Additionally, the encryption key must be automatically rotated every year. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

A.zh: 将数据移至 S3 存储桶。使用 Amazon S3 托管加密密钥 (SSE-S3) 的服务器端加密。使用 SSE-S3 加密密钥的内置密钥轮换行为。
A.en: Move the data to the S3 bucket. Use server-side encryption with Amazon S3 managed encryption keys (SSE-S3). Use the built-in key rotation behavior of SSE-S3 encryption keys.

B.zh: 创建 AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) 客户托管密钥。启用自动密钥轮换。将 S3 存储桶的默认加密行为设置为使用客户管理的 KMS 密钥。将数据移至 S3 存储桶。
B.en: Create an AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) customer managed key. Enable automatic key rotation. Set the S3 bucket’s default encryption behavior to use the customer managed KMS key. Move the data to the S3 bucket.

C.zh: 创建 AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) 客户托管密钥。将 S3 存储桶的默认加密行为设置为使用客户管理的 KMS 密钥。将数据移至 S3 存储桶。每年手动轮换 KMS 密钥。
C.en: Create an AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) customer managed key. Set the S3 bucket’s default encryption behavior to use the customer managed KMS key. Move the data to the S3 bucket. Manually rotate the KMS key every year.

D.zh: 在将数据移至 S3 存储桶之前使用客户密钥材料加密数据。创建不含密钥材料的 AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) 密钥。将客户密钥材料导入到 KMS 密钥中。启用自动密钥轮换。
D.en: Encrypt the data with customer key material before moving the data to the S3 bucket. Create an AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) key without key material. Import the customer key material into the KMS key. Enable automatic key rotation.

A.zh: 正確。技术原因： SSE-S3 加密密钥的内置密钥轮换行为。分析： SSE-S3 是 Amazon S3 的内置加密选项，它使用由 Amazon S3 管理的加密密钥。 SSE-S3 自动管理密钥创建和轮换，无需用户干预。此选项完全满足题目要求，且运行开销最低。
A.en: Correct. Technical reason: the built-in key rotation behavior of SSE-S3 encryption keys. Analysis: SSE-S3 is a built-in encryption option for Amazon S3 that uses encryption keys managed by Amazon S3. SSE-S3 automatically manages key creation and rotation without user intervention. This option fully meets the question requirements and has the lowest operating overhead. Conclusion:.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：存储桶的默认加密行为使用客户管理的 KMS 密钥。将数据移至 S3 存储桶。分析：使用 AWS KMS 客户托管密钥可以实现加密和自动密钥轮换。虽然满足了问题要求，但需要额外配置和管理KMS密钥，增加了操作开销。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: bucket’s default encryption behavior to use the customer managed KMS key. Move the data to the S3 bucket. Analysis: Encryption and automatic key rotation can be achieved using AWS KMS customer managed key. Although it meets the question requirements, it requires additional configuration and management of KMS keys, which increases operational overhead. Conclusion: Wrong.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：使用客户管理的 KMS 密钥的行为。将数据移至 S3 存储桶。每年手动轮换 KMS 密钥。分析：手动轮换KMS密钥会增加操作开销，且不符合问题的「自动轮调」要求。此选项不满足问题所需的最低运维开销。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: behavior to use the customer managed KMS key. Move the data to the S3 bucket. Manually rotate the KMS key every year. Analysis: Manually rotating the KMS key increases operational overhead and does not meet the "automatic rotation" requirement of the question. This option does not meet the minimum operational overhead required by the question. Conclusion: Wrong.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：没有密钥材料的服务 (AWS KMS) 密钥。将客户密钥材料导入到 KMS 密钥中。启用自动密钥轮换。分析：此选项要求用户在迁移数据之前手动加密数据并将客户密钥数据导入KMS。操作复杂，不满足问题要求的「最小操作开销」。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Service (AWS KMS) key without key material. Import the customer key material into the KMS key. Enable automatic key rotation. Analysis: This option requires the user to manually encrypt the data and import the customer key material into the KMS before migrating the data. The operation is complicated and does not meet the "minimum operation overhead" required by the question. Conclusion: Wrong.

answer_text.zh: A. 将数据移至 S3 存储桶。使用 Amazon S3 托管加密密钥 (SSE-S3) 的服务器端加密。使用 SSE-S3 加密密钥的内置密钥轮换行为。
answer_text.en: A. Move the data to the S3 bucket. Use server-side encryption with Amazon S3 managed encryption keys (SSE-S3). Use the built-in key rotation behavior of SSE-S3 encryption keys.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果： A (50%) B (50%)。热门讨论与考点摘要： SSE-S3 加密密钥的内置密钥轮换行为。分析： SSE-S3 是 Amazon S3 的内置加密选项，它使用由 Amazon S3 管理的加密密钥。 SSE-S3 自动管理密钥创建和轮换，无需用户干预。此选项完全满足题目要求，且运行开销最低。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: A (50%) B (50%). Popular discussion and key point summary: the built-in key rotation behavior of SSE-S3 encryption keys. Analysis: SSE-S3 is a built-in encryption option for Amazon S3 that uses encryption keys managed by Amazon S3. SSE-S3 automatically manages key creation and rotation without user intervention. This option fully meets the question requirements and has the lowest operating overhead. Conclusion:.

questions.domain: 領域 2：設計高性能架構 (Design High-Performing Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家金融公司的客户通过发送短信请求与财务顾问预约。在 Amazon EC2 实例上运行的 Web 应用程序接受预约请求。文本消息通过 Web 应用程序发布到 Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) 队列。然后，在 EC2 实例上运行的另一个应用程序会向客户发送会议邀请和会议确认电子邮件。成功安排后，该应用程序会将会议信息保存在 Amazon DynamoDB 数据库中。随着公司的扩张，客户报告说他们的会议邀请需要更长的时间才能送达。解决方案架构师应该建议什么来解决这个问题？
question_text.en: The customers of a finance company request appointments with financial advisors by sending text messages. A web application that runs on Amazon EC2 instances accepts the appointment requests. The text messages are published to an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue through the web application. Another application that runs on EC2 instances then sends meeting invitations and meeting confirmation email messages to the customers. After successful scheduling, this application stores the meeting information in an Amazon DynamoDB database. As the company expands, customers report that their meeting invitations are taking longer to arrive. What should a solutions architect recommend to resolve this issue?

A.zh: 在 DynamoDB 数据库前面添加 DynamoDB Accelerator (DAX) 集群。
A.en: Add a DynamoDB Accelerator (DAX) cluster in front of the DynamoDB database.

B.zh: 在接受预约请求的 Web 应用程序前面添加 Amazon API Gateway API。
B.en: Add an Amazon API Gateway API in front of the web application that accepts the appointment requests.

C.zh: 添加 Amazon CloudFront 分配。将来源设置为接受预约请求的 Web 应用程序。
C.en: Add an Amazon CloudFront distribution. Set the origin as the web application that accepts the appointment requests.

D.zh: 为发送会议邀请的应用程序添加 Auto Scaling 组。配置 Auto Scaling 组以根据 SQS 队列的深度进行扩展。
D.en: Add an Auto Scaling group for the application that sends meeting invitations. Configure the Auto Scaling group to scale based on the depth of the SQS queue.

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因： DAX主要用于加速DynamoDB的读取操作，问题中陈述的问题是会议邀请发送较晚，这与DynamoDB的性能无关。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: DAX is mainly used to speed up the read operation of DynamoDB, and the problem stated in the question is that the meeting invitation is sent late, which has nothing to do with the performance of DynamoDB.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因： API网关可以优化请求的管理，但问题的内核是发送会议邀请的延迟，而不是预订请求的处理速度。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: API Gateway can optimize the management of requests, but the core of the problem is the delay in sending meeting invitations, not the processing speed of reservation requests.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因： CloudFront主要用于内部分发和缓存资源，与发送会议邀请无关。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: CloudFront is mainly used for internal distribution and cache resources, and has nothing to do with sending meeting invitations.

D.zh: 正確。技术原因：通过监控 SQS 队列的流量， Auto Scaling 组可以动态扩展处理会议邀请的 EC2 实例数量，从而减少延迟。
D.en: Correct. Technical reason: By monitoring the traffic of the SQS queue, the Auto Scaling group can dynamically scale the number of EC2 instances that handle meeting invitations, thereby reducing latency.

answer_text.zh: D. 为发送会议邀请的应用程序添加 Auto Scaling 组。配置 Auto Scaling 组以根据 SQS 队列的深度进行扩展。
answer_text.en: D. Add an Auto Scaling group for the application that sends meeting invitations. Configure the Auto Scaling group to scale based on the depth of the SQS queue.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果： D (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：通过监控 SQS 队列的流量， Auto Scaling 组可以动态扩展处理会议邀请的 EC2 实例数量，从而减少延迟。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: D (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: By monitoring the traffic of the SQS queue, the Auto Scaling group can dynamically scale the number of EC2 instances that handle meeting invitations, thereby reducing latency.

questions.domain: 領域 2：設計高性能架構 (Design High-Performing Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家在线零售公司拥有超过 5,000 万活跃客户，每天收到超过 25,000 个订单。该公司收集客户的购买数据并将这些数据保存在 Amazon S3 中。其他客户数据保存在 Amazon RDS 中。该公司希望将所有数据提供给各个团队，以便团队可以进行分析。此解决方案必须提供管理细粒度数据权限的能力，并且必须最大限度地减少运维开销。哪种解决方案可以满足这些要求？
question_text.en: An online retail company has more than 50 million active customers and receives more than 25,000 orders each day. The company collects purchase data for customers and stores this data in Amazon S3. Additional customer data is stored in Amazon RDS. The company wants to make all the data available to various teams so that the teams can perform analytics. The solution must provide the ability to manage fine-grained permissions for the data and must minimize operational overhead. Which solution will meet these requirements?

A.zh: 迁移购买数据以直接写入 Amazon RDS。使用 RDS 访问控制来限制访问。
A.en: Migrate the purchase data to write directly to Amazon RDS. Use RDS access controls to limit access.

B.zh: 安排 AWS Lambda 函数定期将数据从 Amazon RDS 复制到 Amazon S3。创建 AWS Glue 爬网进程。使用 Amazon Athena 查找数据。使用 S3 策略来限制访问。
B.en: Schedule an AWS Lambda function to periodically copy data from Amazon RDS to Amazon S3. Create an AWS Glue crawler. Use Amazon Athena to query the data. Use S3 policies to limit access.

C.zh: 使用 AWS Lake Formation 创建数据湖。创建与 Amazon RDS 的 AWS Glue JDBC 连接。在 Lake Formation 中注册 S3 存储桶。使用 Lake Formation 访问控制来限制访问。
C.en: Create a data lake by using AWS Lake Formation. Create an AWS Glue JDBC connection to Amazon RDS. Register the S3 bucket in Lake Formation. Use Lake Formation access controls to limit access.

D.zh: 创建 Amazon Redshift 集群。安排 AWS Lambda 函数会定期将数据从 Amazon S3 和 Amazon RDS 复制到 Amazon Redshift。使用 Amazon Redshift 访问控制来限制访问。
D.en: Create an Amazon Redshift cluster. Schedule an AWS Lambda function to periodically copy data from Amazon S3 and Amazon RDS to Amazon Redshift. Use Amazon Redshift access controls to limit access.

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：将所有数据迁移到Amazon RDS会导致存储成本增加，且RDS不适合保存大量非结构化数据（例如S3中的数据）。另外， RDS的权限管理不够灵活，无法满足细致的权限需求。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Reason for the Migrating all data to Amazon RDS will result in increased storage costs, and RDS is not suitable for storing large amounts of unstructured data (such as data in S3). In addition, RDS's permission management is not flexible enough to meet fine-grained permission requirements.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：此方案虽然可以实现数据分析，但S3策略的权限管理不够细粒度，无法满足问题的要求。此外，定期复制数据会增加操作复杂性。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: reason: Although this solution can achieve data analysis, the permission management of S3 policies is not fine-grained enough to meet the requirements of the question. Additionally, regularly replicating data increases operational complexity.

C.zh: 正確。技术原因： Lake Formation是专门为构建数据湖而设计的服务，支持细粒度的权限管理。 RDS数据可以通过AWS Glue JDBC连接集成；通过注册S3桶，可以集成S3数据。此方案满足了问题对于权限管理和简化操作的要求。
C.en: Correct. Technical reason: reason: Lake Formation is a service specially designed for building data lakes and supports fine-grained permission management. RDS data can be integrated through AWS Glue JDBC connection; S3 data can be integrated by registering an S3 bucket. This solution meets the requirements of the question for permission management and simplified operations.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：虽然Redshift适合数据分析，但定期复制数据会增加操作复杂度。另外， Redshift的权限管理不如Lake Formation灵活，无法满足细粒度的权限需求。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: reason: Although Redshift is suitable for data analysis, regular copying of data increases operational complexity. In addition, Redshift's permission management is not as flexible as Lake Formation and cannot meet fine-grained permission requirements.

answer_text.zh: C. 使用 AWS Lake Formation 创建数据湖。创建与 Amazon RDS 的 AWS Glue JDBC 连接。在 Lake Formation 中注册 S3 存储桶。使用 Lake Formation 访问控制来限制访问。
answer_text.en: C. Create a data lake by using AWS Lake Formation. Create an AWS Glue JDBC connection to Amazon RDS. Register the S3 bucket in Lake Formation. Use Lake Formation access controls to limit access.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果： C (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：原因： Lake Formation是专门为构建数据湖而设计的服务，支持细粒度的权限管理。 RDS数据可以通过AWS Glue JDBC连接集成；通过注册S3桶，可以集成S3数据。此方案满足了问题对于权限管理和简化操作的要求。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: C (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: reason: Lake Formation is a service specially designed for building data lakes and supports fine-grained permission management. RDS data can be integrated through AWS Glue JDBC connection; S3 data can be integrated by registering an S3 bucket. This solution meets the requirements of the question for permission management and simplified operations.

questions.domain: 領域 4：設計成本優化架構 (Design Cost-Optimized Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司在本地数据中心托管行销网站。该网站由静态文档组成，并在单一服务器上运行。管理员很少更新网站内容，并使用SFTP客户端上传新文档。该公司决定在 AWS 上托管其网站并使用 Amazon CloudFront。该公司的解决方案架构师创建了一个 CloudFront 发行版。解决方案架构师必须为网站托管设计最具成本效益和弹性的架构，以充当 CloudFront 来源。哪种解决方案可以满足这些要求？
question_text.en: A company hosts a marketing website in an on-premises data center. The website consists of static documents and runs on a single server. An administrator updates the website content infrequently and uses an SFTP client to upload new documents. The company decides to host its website on AWS and to use Amazon CloudFront. The company’s solutions architect creates a CloudFront distribution. The solutions architect must design the most cost-effective and resilient architecture for website hosting to serve as the CloudFront origin. Which solution will meet these requirements?

A.zh: 使用 Amazon Lightsail 创建虚拟服务器。在 Lightsail 实例中设置 Web 服务器。使用SFTP客户端上传网站内容。
A.en: Create a virtual server by using Amazon Lightsail. Configure the web server in the Lightsail instance. Upload website content by using an SFTP client.

B.zh: 为 Amazon EC2 实例创建 AWS Auto Scaling 组。使用应用程序负载均衡器。使用SFTP客户端上传网站内容。
B.en: Create an AWS Auto Scaling group for Amazon EC2 instances. Use an Application Load Balancer. Upload website content by using an SFTP client.

C.zh: 创建私有 Amazon S3 存储桶。使用 S3 存储桶策略允许从 CloudFront 来源访问身分识别 (OAI) 进行访问。使用 AWS CLI 上传网站内容。
C.en: Create a private Amazon S3 bucket. Use an S3 bucket policy to allow access from a CloudFront origin access identity (OAI). Upload website content by using the AWS CLI.

D.zh: 创建公用 Amazon S3 存储桶。配置 AWS Transfer for SFTP。配置用于网站托管的 S3 存储桶。使用SFTP客户端上传网站内容。
D.en: Create a public Amazon S3 bucket. Configure AWS Transfer for SFTP. Configure the S3 bucket for website hosting. Upload website content by using the SFTP client.

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因： Lightsail 可用于托管网站，但需要运行虚拟服务器并配置 Web 服务器，这增加了管理复杂性和成本。提出问题的原因需要最具成本效益和最灵活的架构，而 Lightsail 需要连续运行实例，成本更高，而且不像 S3 的静态托管那么简单。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: ness analysis: Lightsail can be used to host websites, but it requires running a virtual server and configuring the web server, which increases management complexity and cost. Reason for the The question requires the most cost-effective and flexible architecture, and Lightsail requires continuous running instances, which is more expensive and not as simple as S3's static hosting.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因： EC2 Auto Scaling 组和 ALB 可以提供高可用性，但这是专为动态内容设计的架构。问题原因明确指出网站是静态内容，使用EC2和ALB会导致不必要的复杂性和高昂的成本。

Security is ensured using OAI. Correct reason: This is the most cost-effective and flexible solution and fully meets the requirements of the question.

questions.domain: 領域 3：設計安全架構 (Design Secure Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司想要管理 Amazon 机器映像 (AMI)。该公司目前将 AMI 复制到创建 AMI 的相同 AWS 区域。该公司需要设计一个应用程序来捕获 AWS API 调用，并在公司账户内调用 Amazon EC2 CreateImage API 操作时发送警报。哪种解决方案能够以最少的运维开销满足这些要求？
question_text.en: A company wants to manage Amazon Machine Images (AMIs). The company currently copies AMIs to the same AWS Region where the AMIs were created. The company needs to design an application that captures AWS API calls and sends alerts whenever the Amazon EC2 CreateImage API operation is called within the company’s account. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

A.zh: 创建 AWS Lambda 函数以查找 AWS CloudTrail 日志并在检测到 CreateImage API 调用时发送警报。
A.en: Create an AWS Lambda function to query AWS CloudTrail logs and to send an alert when a CreateImage API call is detected.

B.zh: 使用 Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) 通知配置 AWS CloudTrail，该通知会在更新的日志发送至 Amazon S3 时发生。使用 Amazon Athena 创建新数据表并在检测到 API 调用时对 CreateImage 进行查找。
B.en: Configure AWS CloudTrail with an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) notification that occurs when updated logs are sent to Amazon S3. Use Amazon Athena to create a new table and to query on CreateImage when an API call is detected.

C.zh: 为 CreateImage API 调用创建 Amazon EventBridge (Amazon CloudWatch Events) 规则。将目标配置为 Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) 主题，以便在检测到 CreateImage API 调用时发送警报。
C.en: Create an Amazon EventBridge (Amazon CloudWatch Events) rule for the CreateImage API call. Configure the target as an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) topic to send an alert when a CreateImage API call is detected.

D.zh: 将 Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) FIFO 队列配置为 AWS CloudTrail 日志的目标。创建一个 AWS Lambda 函数，以便在检测到 CreateImage API 调用时向 Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) 主题发送警报。
D.en: Configure an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) FIFO queue as a target for AWS CloudTrail logs. Create an AWS Lambda function to send an alert to an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) topic when a CreateImage API call is detected.

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：虽然可以通过 Lambda 查找 CloudTrail 日志来检测 API 调用，但这种方法需要定期运行 Lambda 函数来扫描日志，从而增加了操作开销。另外，这种方法不是即时的，可能会有延迟。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: While it is possible to query CloudTrail logs via Lambda to detect API calls, this approach requires running a Lambda function periodically to scan the logs, adding operational overhead. In addition, this method is not real-time and there may be delays.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：此方法依赖CloudTrail将日志保存到S3，然后使用Athena查找日志。此方法需要手动配置Athena表，查找过程可能会有延迟。运维费用较高，不符合「最低运维费用」要求。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This method relies on CloudTrail to store the logs to S3 and then use Athena to query the logs. This method requires manual configuration of the Athena table, and the query process may be delayed. The operating overhead is high and does not meet the "minimum operating overhead" requirement.

C.zh: 正確。技术原因： EventBridge 可以直接截取 CreateImage API 调用事件并将其发送至 SNS 主题以触发警报。这种方法是即时的并且具有最小的操作开销，因为不需要额外的日志查找或保存配置。
C.en: Correct. Technical reason: EventBridge can capture CreateImage API call events directly and send them to an SNS topic to trigger an alert. This approach is real-time and has minimal operational overhead since no additional log queries or storage configuration are required.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：此方法需要配置SQS队列和Lambda函数，增加了操作复杂度。另外，依赖CloudTrail日志可能会造成延迟，而且不如EventBridge即时。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This method requires configuring an SQS queue and Lambda function, increasing operational complexity. In addition, relying on CloudTrail logs may cause delays and is not as real-time as EventBridge.

answer_text.zh: C. 为 CreateImage API 调用创建 Amazon EventBridge (Amazon CloudWatch Events) 规则。将目标配置为 Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) 主题，以便在检测到 CreateImage API 调用时发送警报。
answer_text.en: C. Create an Amazon EventBridge (Amazon CloudWatch Events) rule for the CreateImage API call. Configure the target as an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) topic to send an alert when a CreateImage API call is detected.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果： C (77%) A (15%) 5%。热门讨论与考点摘要： EventBridge 可以直接截取 CreateImage API 调用事件并将其发送至 SNS 主题以触发警报。这种方法是即时的并且具有最小的操作开销，因为不需要额外的日志查找或保存配置。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: C (77%) A (15%) 5%. Popular discussion and key point summary: EventBridge can capture CreateImage API call events directly and send them to an SNS topic to trigger an alert. This approach is real-time and has minimal operational overhead since no additional log queries or storage configuration are required.

questions.domain: 領域 2：設計高性能架構 (Design High-Performing Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司拥有一个异步 API，用于接收用户请求，并根据请求类型将请求分派到适当的微服务进行处理。该公司正在使用 Amazon API Gateway 部署 API 前端，并使用 AWS Lambda 函数调用 Amazon DynamoDB 来保存用户请求，然后再将其分派到处理微服务。该公司在预算允许的范围内配置了尽可能多的 DynamoDB 吞吐量，但该公司仍然遇到可用性问题并且正在丢失用户请求。解决方案架构师应该如何解决这个问题而不影响现有用户？
question_text.en: A company owns an asynchronous API that is used to ingest user requests and, based on the request type, dispatch requests to the appropriate microservice for processing. The company is using Amazon API Gateway to deploy the API front end, and an AWS Lambda function that invokes Amazon DynamoDB to store user requests before dispatching them to the processing microservices. The company provisioned as much DynamoDB throughput as its budget allows, but the company is still experiencing availability issues and is losing user requests. What should a solutions architect do to address this issue without impacting existing users?

A.zh: 通过服务器端限制在 API 网关上添加限制。
A.en: Add throttling on the API Gateway with server-side throttling limits.

B.zh: 使用 DynamoDB Accelerator (DAX) 和 Lambda 缓冲对 DynamoDB 的写入。
B.en: Use DynamoDB Accelerator (DAX) and Lambda to buffer writes to DynamoDB.

C.zh: 在 DynamoDB 中为包含用户要求的表格创建二级索引。
C.en: Create a secondary index in DynamoDB for the table with the user requests.

D.zh: 使用 Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) 队列和 Lambda 缓冲对 DynamoDB 的写入。
D.en: Use the Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue and Lambda to buffer writes to DynamoDB.

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因： API网关的限流虽然可以减少请求数量，但会直接影响用户体验，导致用户请求被拒绝或延迟。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Analysis: Although the throttling limit of API Gateway can reduce the number of requests, it will directly affect the user experience, causing user requests to be rejected or delayed.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因： DAX主要用于加速读取操作，而非写入操作。问题中指出的问题是写入吞吐量不足。因为DAX是用来解决无条纹问题的，所以选项是错的。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Analysis: DAX is mainly used to speed up read operations, not write operations. The problem stated in the question is insufficient write throughput. Because DAX is used to solve the problem without stripes, the options are wrong.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：二级索引用于优化查找性能，与写入吞吐量无关。创建二级索引并不能缓解写入压力，因为。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Analysis: Secondary indexes are used to optimize query performance and have nothing to do with write throughput. Creating a secondary index will not relieve the writing pressure because of the.

D.zh: 正確。技术原因：使用Amazon SQS作为缓冲队列可以有效分散写入压力，将用户请求暂时保存在队列中，然后通过Lambda函数逐步写入DynamoDB。 对 S3 存储桶的
D.en: Correct. Technical reason: Analysis: Using Amazon SQS as a buffer queue can effectively disperse the writing pressure, temporarily store user requests in the queue, and then gradually write them to DynamoDB through the Lambda function.

answer_text.zh: D. 使用 Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) 队列和 Lambda 缓冲对 DynamoDB 的写入。
answer_text.en: D. Use the Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue and Lambda to buffer writes to DynamoDB.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果： D (99%) 1。热门讨论与考点摘要：分析：使用Amazon SQS作为缓冲队列可以有效分散写入压力，将用户请求暂时保存在队列中，然后通过Lambda函数逐步写入DynamoDB。 对 S3 存储桶的
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: D (99%) 1. Popular discussion and key point summary: Analysis: Using Amazon SQS as a buffer queue can effectively disperse the writing pressure, temporarily store user requests in the queue, and then gradually write them to DynamoDB through the Lambda function.

questions.domain: 領域 3：設計安全架構 (Design Secure Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司需要将数据从 Amazon EC2 实例移至 Amazon S3 存储桶。公司必须确保没有 API 调用和数据通过公共互联网路由进行路由。只有 EC2 实例有权将数据上传到 S3 存储桶。哪种解决方案可以满足这些要求？
question_text.en: A company needs to move data from an Amazon EC2 instance to an Amazon S3 bucket. The company must ensure that no API calls and no data are routed through public internet routes. Only the EC2 instance can have access to upload data to the S3 bucket. Which solution will meet these requirements?

A.zh: 在 EC2 实例所在的子网中为 Amazon S3 创建接口 VPC 终端节点。将资源策略附加到 S3 存储桶，以仅允许 EC2 实例的 IAM 角色进行访问。
A.en: Create an interface VPC endpoint for Amazon S3 in the subnet where the EC2 instance is located. Attach a resource policy to the S3 bucket to only allow the EC2 instance’s IAM role for access.

B.zh: 在 EC2 实例所在的可用区中为 Amazon S3 创建网关 VPC 终端节点。将适当的安全组附加到端点。将资源策略附加到 S3 存储桶，以仅允许 EC2 实例的 IAM 角色进行访问。
B.en: Create a gateway VPC endpoint for Amazon S3 in the Availability Zone where the EC2 instance is located. Attach appropriate security groups to the endpoint. Attach a resource policy to the S3 bucket to only allow the EC2 instance’s IAM role for access.

C.zh: 从 EC2 实例内部运行 nslookup 工具以获取 S3 存储桶的服务 API 端点的私有 IP 地址。在 VPC 路由表中创建路​​由，为 EC2 实例提供对 S3 存储桶的访问权限。将资源策略附加到 S3 存储桶，以仅允许 EC2 实例的 IAM 角色进行访问。
C.en: Run the nslookup tool from inside the EC2 instance to obtain the private IP address of the S3 bucket’s service API endpoint. Create a route in the VPC route table to provide the EC2 instance with access to the S3 bucket. Attach a resource policy to the S3 bucket to only allow the EC2 instance’s IAM role for access.

D.zh: 使用 AWS 提供的公开可用的 ip-ranges.json 文档获取 S3 存储桶的服务 API 端点的私有 IP 地址。在 VPC 路由表中创建路​​由，为 EC2 实例提供对 S3 存储桶的访问权限。将资源策略附加到 S3 存储桶，以仅允许 EC2 实例的 IAM 角色进行访问。
D.en: Use the AWS provided, publicly available ip-ranges.json file to obtain the private IP address of the S3 bucket’s service API endpoint. Create a route in the VPC route table to provide the EC2 instance with access to the S3 bucket. Attach a resource policy to the S3 bucket to only allow the EC2 instance’s IAM role for access.

A.zh: 正確。技术原因：策略仅允许 EC2 实例的 IAM 角色进行访问。分析：接口 VPC 终端节点允许通过专用网络访问 Amazon S3，而无需通过公共互联网。资源策略可以将对 S3 存储桶的访问权限限制为仅特定 IAM 角色（EC2 实例的 IAM 角色）。此选项完全满足问题的要求：避免公共网络访问并限制访问权限。
A.en: Correct. Technical reason: policy to the S3 bucket to only allow the EC2 instance’s IAM role for access. Analysis: The Interface VPC Endpoint allows access to Amazon S3 over a private network without going through the public internet. Resource policies can restrict access to S3 buckets to only specific IAM roles (IAM roles for EC2 instances). This option fully meets the requirements of the question: avoid public Internet access and limit access rights.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：将适当的安全组加入端点。将资源策略附加到 S3 存储桶，以仅允许 EC2 实例的 IAM 角色进行访问。分析：网关 VPC 终端节点可与 Amazon S3 和 DynamoDB 搭配使用，但它是基于路由表而不是基于子网的接口终端节点。问题要求「在子网中创建终端节点」，而不是在可用区中创建终端节点，因此该选项不能完全满足问题要求。此外，安全组无法直接附加到网关 VPC 终端节点。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: appropriate security groups to the endpoint. Attach a resource policy to the S3 bucket to only allow the EC2 instance’s IAM role for access. Analysis: Gateway VPC Endpoint works with Amazon S3 and DynamoDB, but it is based on route tables rather than subnet-based interface endpoints. The question requires "Create an endpoint in a subnet" instead of creating an endpoint in an Availability Zone, so this option does not fully meet the question requirements. Additionally, security groups cannot be attached directly to Gateway VPC Endpoint.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：端点。在 VPC 路由表中创建路​​由，为 EC2 实例提供对 S3 存储桶的访问权限。将资源策略附加到 S3 存储桶，以仅允许 EC2 实例的 IAM 角色进行访问。分析：使用nslookup工具获取S3的私有IP地址并手动创建路由表条目是不可行的，因为S3服务端点是动态的，不能依赖这种方式。来实现访问。这种方法很复杂且不符合 AWS 最佳实践。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: endpoint. Create a route in the VPC route table to provide the EC2 instance with access to the S3 bucket. Attach a resource policy to the S3 bucket to only allow the EC2 instance’s IAM role for access. Analysis: Using the nslookup tool to obtain S3’s private IP address and manually create the route table entry is not feasible because the S3 service endpoint is dynamic and cannot be relied upon this way. to achieve access. This approach is complex and inconsistent with AWS best practices.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因： API 端点。在 VPC 路由表中创建路​​由，为 EC2 实例提供对 S3 存储桶的访问权限。将资源策略附加到 S3 存储桶，以仅允许 EC2 实例的 IAM 角色进行访问。分析： ip-ranges.json文档提供了AWS服务的公共IP地址范围，而不是私有IP地址。此方法无法实现通过私网访问S3服务。该选项不符合题目要求。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: API endpoint. Create a route in the VPC route table to provide the EC2 instance with access to the S3 bucket. Attach a resource policy to the S3 bucket to only allow the EC2 instance’s IAM role for access. Analysis: The ip-ranges.json file provides the public IP address range of the AWS service, not the private IP address. This method cannot achieve access to S3 services through private networks. This option does not meet the question requirements.

answer_text.zh: A. 在 EC2 实例所在的子网中为 Amazon S3 创建接口 VPC 终端节点。将资源策略附加到 S3 存储桶，以仅允许 EC2 实例的 IAM 角色进行访问。
answer_text.en: A. Create an interface VPC endpoint for Amazon S3 in the subnet where the EC2 instance is located. Attach a resource policy to the S3 bucket to only allow the EC2 instance’s IAM role for access.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果： A (56%) B (44%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：策略仅允许 EC2 实例的 IAM 角色进行访问。分析：接口 VPC 终端节点允许通过专用网络访问 Amazon S3，而无需通过公共互联网。资源策略可以将对 S3 存储桶的访问权限限制为仅特定 IAM 角色（EC2 实例的 IAM 角色）。此选项完全满足问题的要求：避免公共网络访问并限制访问权限。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: A (56%) B (44%). Popular discussion and key point summary: policy to the S3 bucket to only allow the EC2 instance’s IAM role for access. Analysis: The Interface VPC Endpoint allows access to Amazon S3 over a private network without going through the public internet. Resource policies can restrict access to S3 buckets to only specific IAM roles (IAM roles for EC2 instances). This option fully meets the requirements of the question: avoid public Internet access and limit access rights.

questions.domain: 領域 1：設計彈性架構 (Design Resilient Architectures)

question_text.zh: A solutions architect is designing the architecture of a new application being deployed to the AWS Cloud. The application will run on Amazon EC2 On-Demand Instances and will automatically scale a downcple scale a [quulity seal alt. throughout the day. An Application Load Balancer (ALB) will handle the load distribution. The architecture needs to support distributed session data management. The company is willing to make changes to code if needed。 distributed session data management?
question_text.en: A solutions architect is designing the architecture of a new application being deployed to the AWS Cloud. The application will run on Amazon EC2 On-Demand Instances and will automatically scale across multiple Availability Zones. The EC2 instances will scale up and down frequently throughout the day. An Application Load Balancer (ALB) will handle the load distribution. The architecture needs to support distributed session data management. The company is willing to make changes to code if needed. What should the solutions architect do to ensure that the architecture supports distributed session data management?

A.zh: 使用 Amazon ElastiCache 管理和保存会话数据。
A.en: Use Amazon ElastiCache to manage and store session data.

B.zh: 使用 ALB 的会话亲和性（黏性会话）来管理会话数据。
B.en: Use session affinity (sticky sessions) of the ALB to manage session data.

C.zh: 使用 AWS Systems Manager 中的会话管理器来管理会话。
C.en: Use Session Manager from AWS Systems Manager to manage the session.

D.zh: 使用 AWS Security Token Service (AWS STS) 中的 GetSessionToken API 作业来管理会话。
D.en: Use the GetSessionToken API operation in AWS Security Token Service (AWS STS) to manage the session.

A.zh: 正確。技术原因： Amazon ElastiCache 是一种高性能、低延迟的分布式缓存服务，适合保存会话数据并支持分布式架构。它支持上下游EC2实例，并且可以通过Redis或Memcached共享会话数据。
A.en: Correct. Technical reason: Amazon ElastiCache is a high-performance, low-latency distributed cache service suitable for storing session data and supporting distributed architectures. It supports upstream and downstream EC2 instances, and can share session data through Redis or Memcached.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因： Sticky Sessions 会将用户请求导向至特定的 EC2 实例，但这不是 Hatamasa 的分布式会话管理解决方案。当 EC2 实例频繁启动和关闭时，黏性会话可能会导致会话丢失或不一致。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Sticky Sessions will target user requests to specific EC2 instances, but this is not Hatamasa's distributed session management solution. Sticky Sessions can cause session loss or inconsistency when EC2 instances are frequently brought up and down.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因： Session Manager 是一个用于管理 EC2 实例的远程会话工具，与应用程序会话数据管理无关。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Session Manager is a remote session tool used to manage EC2 instances and has nothing to do with application session data management.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因： GetSessionToken API 用于产生临时安全性凭证，与应用程序的会话数据管理无关。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: The GetSessionToken API is used to generate temporary security credentials and has nothing to do with the application's session data management.

answer_text.zh: A. 使用 Amazon ElastiCache 管理和保存会话数据。
answer_text.en: A. Use Amazon ElastiCache to manage and store session data.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果： A (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要： Amazon ElastiCache 是一种高性能、低延迟的分布式缓存服务，适合保存会话数据并支持分布式架构。它支持上下游EC2实例，并且可以通过Redis或Memcached共享会话数据。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: A (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Amazon ElastiCache is a high-performance, low-latency distributed cache service suitable for storing session data and supporting distributed architectures. It supports upstream and downstream EC2 instances, and can share session data through Redis or Memcached.

questions.domain: 領域 2：設計高性能架構 (Design High-Performing Architectures)

question_text.zh: A company offers a food delivery service that is growing rapidly. Because of the growth, the company’s order processing system is experiencing scaling problems during peak traffsing system is experiencing scaling problems during peak traffic hours.实例，用于从应用程序收集订单 • 在 Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling 组中运行的另一组 EC2 实例，用于履行订单 订单收集过程发生得很快，但订单履行过程可能需要更长的时间。 Data must not be lost because of a scaling event.解决方案架构师必须确保订单收集流程和订单履行流程都能在高峰流量时段适当扩展。 The solution must optimize utilization of the company’s AWS resources.哪一种解决方案符合这些要求？
question_text.en: A company offers a food delivery service that is growing rapidly. Because of the growth, the company’s order processing system is experiencing scaling problems during peak traffic hours. The current architecture includes the following: • A group of Amazon EC2 instances that run in an Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling group to collect orders from the application • Another group of EC2 instances that run in an Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling group to fulfill orders The order collection process occurs quickly, but the order fulfillment process can take longer. Data must not be lost because of a scaling event. A solutions architect must ensure that the order collection process and the order fulfillment process can both scale properly during peak traffic hours. The solution must optimize utilization of the company’s AWS resources. Which solution meets these requirements?

A.zh: 使用 Amazon CloudWatch 指标监控 Auto Scaling 组中每个实例的 CPU。根据峰值工作负载值配置每个 Auto Scaling 组的最小容量。
A.en: Use Amazon CloudWatch metrics to monitor the CPU of each instance in the Auto Scaling groups. Configure each Auto Scaling group’s minimum capacity according to peak workload values.

B.zh: 使用 Amazon CloudWatch 指标监控 Auto Scaling 组中每个实例的 CPU。配置 CloudWatch 警报以调用 Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) 主题，该主题根据需要创建其他 Auto Scaling 组。
B.en: Use Amazon CloudWatch metrics to monitor the CPU of each instance in the Auto Scaling groups. Configure a CloudWatch alarm to invoke an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) topic that creates additional Auto Scaling groups on demand.

C.zh: 设置两个 Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) 队列：一个用于订单收集，另一个用于订单履行。配置 EC2 实例以轮询其各自的队列。根据队列发送的通知扩展 Auto Scaling 组。
C.en: Provision two Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queues: one for order collection and another for order fulfillment. Configure the EC2 instances to poll their respective queue. Scale the Auto Scaling groups based on notifications that the queues send.

D.zh: 设置两个 Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) 队列：一个用于订单收集，另一个用于订单履行。配置 EC2 实例以轮询其各自的队列。根据每个实例的积压计算创建一个指标。根据此指标扩展 Auto Scaling 组。
D.en: Provision two Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queues: one for order collection and another for order fulfillment. Configure the EC2 instances to poll their respective queue. Create a metric based on a backlog per instance calculation. Scale the Auto Scaling groups based on this metric.

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：无法优化资源利用，因为它没有考虑订单处理的动态需求。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Stripless optimization of resource utilization because it does not take into account the dynamic needs of order processing.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：创建额外的 Auto Scaling 组并不是解决订单瓶颈问题的最佳方法。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Creating additional Auto Scaling groups is not the best way to solve the order bottleneck problem.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：一个用于订单收集，另一个用于订单履行。配置 EC2 实例以轮询其各自的队列。根据队列发送的通知扩展 Auto Scaling 组。部分正确：使用SQS队列可以有效分离订单收集和订单完成的流程。仅根据队列发送的通知扩展 Auto Scaling 组可能无法正确处理压力问题。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: One for order collection and the other for order fulfillment. Configure EC2 instances to poll their respective queues. Scale the Auto Scaling group based on notifications sent by the queue. Partially true: Using SQS queues can effectively separate the flow of order collection and order completion. Expanding the Auto Scaling group based only on notifications sent by the queue may not correctly handle the stress issue.

D.zh: 正確。技术原因：一个用于订单收集，另一个用于订单履行。配置 EC2 实例以轮询其各自的队列。根据每个实例的积压计算创建一个指标。根据此指标扩展 Auto Scaling 组。 True：可选择通过 SQS 队列分离订单收集和订单履行流程，并根据压力表动态扩展 Auto Scaling 组。优化资源利用率，同时确保订单不会因规模问题而丢失。
D.en: Correct. Technical reason: One for order collection and the other for order fulfillment. Configure EC2 instances to poll their respective queues. Create a metric based on the backlog calculation for each instance. Scale the Auto Scaling group based on this metric. True: Option to separate order collection and order fulfillment flows through SQS queues and dynamically expand Auto Scaling groups based on pressure gauges. Optimized resource utilization while ensuring orders are not lost due to scaling issues.

answer_text.zh: D. 设置两个 Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) 队列：一个用于订单收集，另一个用于订单履行。配置 EC2 实例以轮询其各自的队列。根据每个实例的积压计算创建一个指标。根据此指标扩展 Auto Scaling 组。
answer_text.en: D. Provision two Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queues: one for order collection and another for order fulfillment. Configure the EC2 instances to poll their respective queue. Create a metric based on a backlog per instance calculation. Scale the Auto Scaling groups based on this metric.
